LAW AND MOTION 301, AMENDED NOTICE OF MTN TO CONSOLIDATE CASES. MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED. A R G U E D AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING -- PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE CASES IS GRANTED ON CONDITION THAT PLAINTIFF IS PRESENTED FOR FULL DEPOSITION AND ALL PLEADINGS, DISMISSALS, DISCOVERY AND RESPONSES AFTER APRIL 30, 2002 ARE PRODUCED. COURT DETERMINED THAT A TIME LIMIT ON THE DEPOSITION IS REASONABLE. COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER AND IF THERE IS NO AGREEMENT, THEN COUNSEL MAY GO TO THE DISCOVERY DEPARTMENT FOR RESOLUTION. HON. A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II

Off Calendar Aug-01-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anna M. Costa, Esq. of PAUL & HANLEY, LLP 510/559-9980 ext 161 for Plaintiff moving to consolidate this action with Action No. 407572
John P. Katerndahl, Esq. of HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP 288-9800 for defendant JOHN CRANE INC. opposing to the motion to consolidate
Howard B. Kim, Esq. of BURNHAM BROWN for defendant NIBCO INC.
* A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- Plaintiff's motion to consolidate cases is granted on condition that Plaintiff is presented for full deposition and all pleadings, dismissals, discovery and responses after April 30, 2002 are produced. Court determined that a time limit on the deposition is reasonable. Counsel to meet and confer and if there is no agreement, then counsel may go to the Discovery Department for resolution. (AJR/301/md) Patricia M. Evezich, CSR 1193
